summary:
The absence of wheezing alongside emerging lethargy indicates severe deteriorating asthma, potentially due to complete airway obstructions leading to hypoxic brain injury. This combination signals immediate danger of respiratory failure necessitating urgent intubation rather than continued medical management alone. 

Note: Do NOT include any commentary about whether the summary matches reality—only summarize what the agent said.

The absence of wheezing together with lethargy suggests profound airway obstruction and potential hypoxia causing impaired cerebral function. These findings indicate rapid disease progression toward respiratory failure, warranting prompt intubation instead of continuing standard care.
